Autonomous Driving.fact: Tesla FSD supervised has a crash rate of 1 per 5 million miles vs US national average of 1 per 600,000 miles — roughly 8x safer than human drivers
Tesla's global FSD fleet has surpassed 8.44 billion cumulative miles driven
Lemonade Insurance offers 50% discount to FSD users, confirming FSD drivers are 2x safer than human drivers; expanded from Arizona to Oregon with 8 more states planned
Tesla fleet generates over 20 million miles of real-world visual data per day for AI training
FSD supervised approved for launch in the Netherlands around March 20, 2026 — Tesla's first European FSD market
FSD is also active in UAE, Korea, Australia, and China as of early March 2026
Tesla discontinued outright FSD purchases in favor of subscription-only model, signaling expectation of significant value increase
NHTSA held first-ever National AV Safety Forum on March 10, 2026; Secretary Duffy announced NHTSA will revise FMVSS to accommodate vehicles without steering wheels or pedals
U.S. Congress advanced the Self-Drive Act through committee with potential passage by end of summer 2026
2022 NHTSA ruling (Occupant Protection Final Rule) eliminated requirement for steering wheels and pedals for fully autonomous vehicles meeting crashworthiness standards
Current US autonomous vehicle exemption cap is 2,500 units, with proposed congressional increase to approximately 90,000-95,000 vehicles per year
Tesla VP Ashok Elluswami stated at Scale ML 2026: 'The self-driving problem is not a sensor problem. It's an AI problem. The cameras have enough information already.'
Former Huawei head of autonomous driving publicly stated Tesla FSD is 'generations ahead' of Chinese competitors
Tesla NHTSA robotaxi incident report Jan 15 - Feb 15 showed only 1 incident (low-speed rear-end at ~1 mph); cumulative total of 15 reported incidents since program began
Japan Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism approved regulatory measures allowing FSD activation via software updates
New UNECE autonomous driving framework adopted January 2026 replaced rigid prescriptive rules with outcome-based safety case approach

Robotaxi.fact: First Cybercab rolled off production line at Giga Texas on February 17, 2026, ahead of planned April start-of-production
Cybercab has no steering wheel, no pedals, only 2 seats; designed exclusively for autonomous operation with inductive charging
Cybercab confirmed at under $30,000 retail price before end of 2026; production cost target approximately $15,000-$25,000 at scale
Cybercab uses Tesla's 'unboxed' manufacturing process targeting one unit every 10 seconds at full scale
Over 100 Cybercabs built by mid-March with units shipped for crash testing and city testing
Tesla robotaxi fleet grew to 372 vehicles by mid-February 2026 (283 Bay Area, 89 Austin), with 7 operating fully unsupervised
Tesla plans robotaxi expansion to 7 additional cities by end of H1 2026: Dallas, Houston, Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Las Vegas
Austin robotaxi pricing: $3 base rate, $1.40/mile, with surge pricing up to $2/mile at night — approximately one-third of Uber/Waymo rates
Cybercab production estimates for 2026: 10,000-30,000 units (conservative) to 40,000 (optimistic); using Cybertruck benchmark of 28,000 units in first 3 quarters
Estimated ~$4 billion in Cybercab manufacturing capex committed; cost per unit at 100,000 volume ~$25,000, at 3,000 volume ~$200,000
Single robotaxi estimated to generate $69,000-$93,000 annually in margin, equivalent to margin from selling 12 Model Y vehicles per year
Tesla robotaxi app expanded to Android (version 26.2.0)
Waymo operates in 10 US cities with ~3,000 vehicles doing ~400,000 rides/week but loses money beyond ~500 vehicles per city; raised $16 billion, remains unprofitable
Waymo had 97 NHTSA-reported incidents in Jan 15 - Feb 15 period vs Tesla's 1 incident
Nvidia-Uber robotaxi launch planned LA/SF H1 2027, 28 cities by 2028

Humanoid Bots.fact: Tesla targeting 50,000 Optimus units production in 2026; Fremont Model S/X lines being converted to Optimus manufacturing
Optimus Gen 3 reveal imminent (Q1 2026); production run rate target of 1 million units per year from Fremont
Optimus V3 target price: $20,000-$30,000; Elon confirmed V4 design is locked down
Musk plans to place 20,000-30,000 Optimus robots in gymnasium-style environment for real-world physics AI training
Optimus hands feature 22-26 degrees of freedom vs Boston Dynamics Atlas's 3-finger gripper
Giga Texas North Campus being prepared for dedicated Optimus factory with 8 million sq ft and 10 million unit annual capacity target (Phase 2, 2027)
Model S and X production ends Q2 2026; Fremont lines being converted for Optimus production
Digital Optimus (Macrohard) announced as joint Tesla/xAI project: two-brain AI system (Tesla AI4 chip + Grok) for automating screen-based white-collar work
Tesla AI4 chip costs $650 per unit; each AI4-equipped Tesla contains two chips and can run two Digital Optimus instances simultaneously while parked
Over 4 million Tesla vehicles equipped with AI4 chip represent a distributed compute network for agentic AI work

Energy.fact: Tesla Megapack factory in Brookshire, Texas: $200 million invested, 1.6 million sq ft, production targeted late 2026, revenue projection $8-10 billion annually at full ramp
Tesla and LG Energy signed $4.3 billion agreement for LFP prismatic battery cell factory in Lansing, Michigan; production start 2027; 50 GWh annual capacity
LG-Tesla deal battery manufacturing tax credits estimated at $2.25 billion per year at full ramp
Tesla Supercharger network surpassed 75,000 stalls globally; delivered 6.7 TWh of energy in 2025
V4 Supercharger cabinets in production at Giga New York; supports 500 kW for cars and 1.2 MW for Semi
Tesla lithium refinery in Corpus Christi, Texas operational as of January 2026 — largest in the US; targeting 50 GWh of battery-grade lithium annually
Giga Nevada battery output estimated at ~80 GWh; Reno LFP factory producing 10 GWh; Giga Texas beginning 4680 production at ~10 GWh
Giga Nevada 4680 expansion planned: 100 GWh capacity at full buildout with $3.6 billion additional investment
Supercharger stations being converted into distributed AI compute nodes leveraging 7 GW of available power globally

Electric Vehicles.fact: Tesla US EV market share rebounded to 61% in January 2026
Tesla is in its 13th consecutive month of year-over-year sales decline in Europe as of early 2026
Cybertruck dual motor price dropped from $80,000 to $59,990; delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 due to demand surge
Tesla Semi spec: 500-mile long range, 325-mile standard range, 1.2 MW charging, 1.7 kWh/mile efficiency
Tesla Semi estimated price: $240,000-$300,000; volume production ramping at Giga Nevada in 2026
Model Y Long (YL) 6-seat variant launched in China, now in Australia/New Zealand
New Roadster unveil expected late April 2026; specs include 0-60 under 2 seconds, 250+ mph, 620-mile range
Model S and Model X production ends in Q2 2026, Fremont lines repurposed for Optimus

Financials.fact: Tesla committed over $20 billion in capex for 2026, up from ~$8.5 billion prior year (135% YoY increase); self-funded from $44 billion cash balance
Tesla 2025 free cash flow was $6.2 billion (up from $3.58 billion in 2024)
Tesla services revenue growing 40% annually; at full FSD subscriber penetration (4.4M) software revenue alone could exceed $5B/year
Terrafab semiconductor fabrication project announced; estimated cost $20-$25 billion; targeting 1 million wafers/month by 2030
Tesla doubled AI chip order with Samsung to 40,000 wafers/month; original contract worth $17 billion over 8 years
Elon Musk purchased ~2.5 million Tesla shares for ~$1 billion on September 15, 2025 at average price just under $390
Major institutional Tesla purchases in Q4 2025: Norges Bank $11.8B new position, Geode Capital ~$20B, Renaissance Technologies $189M
Tesla converted $2 billion xAI investment into SpaceX equity ahead of rumored SpaceX IPO at $1.5-$1.75 trillion valuation
Giga Texas adding 5.2 million sq ft by end of 2026; total facility over 11 million sq ft

Market & Competition.fact: Global EV sales grew 29% in 2024 to 14.2 million vehicles (18% of all new cars)
Honda reported first annual loss in nearly 70 years: $15.7 billion write-down tied to EV business
Stellantis wrote off ~$980 million Canadian battery plant investment (sold for $100); total EV write-downs ~$21.5 billion
Ford wrote down ~$6-7 billion in EV losses; GM ~$12-15 billion in EV write-downs
BYD deployed 1.5 MW flash charging (10% to 97% in 9 minutes) on a $22,000 vehicle
BYD expanding into Europe via Hungary factory; secured 100,000 vehicle orders from Argentina and Mexico
Xpeng licensed vision-only XIMO 2.0 autonomous driving system to Volkswagen — first large-scale export of Chinese AV tech
BMW removing Level 3 autonomy from refreshed 7 Series; Mercedes previously withdrew its Level 3 system
Rivian R2 pricing: performance ~$58,000 (spring 2026), standard RWD $48,000 (2027); CEO said sensor simplification may not happen until 2038-2040
China car exports reached 6.4 million vehicles, surpassing Japan (4.2M), Germany (3.18M), and US (1.3M)
